# @jswork/react-echarts

> React wrapper component for Apache ECharts - a powerful charting library.

## Installation

```bash
npm install @jswork/react-echarts
# or
yarn add @jswork/react-echarts
```

## Peer Dependencies

This package requires:
- `react` >= 18.2.0
- `react-dom` >= 18.2.0
- `@jswork/event-mitt` ^2.0.9
- `@jswork/harmony-events` ^1.2.7

## Basic Usage

```tsx
import ReactEcharts from '@jswork/react-echarts';

function App() {
  const option = {
    title: { text: 'ECharts Demo' },
    tooltip: {},
    xAxis: { data: ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D', 'E'] },
    yAxis: {},
    series: [{ name: 'Sales', type: 'bar', data: [5, 20, 36, 10, 10] }]
  };

  return (
    <ReactEcharts
      style={{ width: '100%', height: '400px' }}
      option={option}
      onReady={({ chart, echarts }) => {
        console.log('Chart instance:', chart);
        console.log('ECharts object:', echarts);
      }}
    />
  );
}
```

## API Reference

### ReactEcharts Component Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `name` | `string` | `'@'` | Identity name for event bus integration |
| `className` | `string` | - | Extended CSS class name |
| `option` | `EChartOption` | `{}` | ECharts configuration option (required) |
| `initOptions` | `object` | `{}` | ECharts initialization options |
| `scriptURL` | `string` | CDN URL | URL to load ECharts script |
| `onReady` | `function` | - | Callback when chart is initialized |
| ...standard HTML div props | - | - | All standard `React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>` are supported |

#### initOptions Details

```typescript
initOptions?: {
  devicePixelRatio?: number;   // Pixel ratio
  renderer?: string;           // 'canvas' or 'svg'
  width?: number | string;     // Chart width
  height?: number | string;    // Chart height
}
```

#### onReady Callback

```typescript
type OnReadyCallback = (params: { chart: ECharts; echarts: any }) => void;
```

- `chart`: The initialized ECharts instance
- `echarts`: The global ECharts object for advanced usage

### useCommand Hook

A hook for interacting with the component via event bus:

```tsx
import { useCommand } from '@jswork/react-echarts';

function MyComponent() {
  const { loadEcharts } = useCommand(); // uses default name '@'
  // or custom name: useCommand('myChart')

  const handleClick = () => {
    loadEcharts(); // triggers chart reload
  };

  return <button onClick={handleClick}>Reload Chart</button>;
}
```

## Component Features

### Auto Resize

The component automatically resizes when its container size changes using `ResizeObserver`. No manual resize calls needed.

### Lazy Loading

ECharts script is loaded lazily from CDN (default: `https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/echarts/5.4.0/echarts.min.js`). You can customize the URL via `scriptURL` prop.

### Loading Spinner

Shows a loading spinner while ECharts is being initialized. Styled via `.react-echarts__spinner` class.

### Smart Re-rendering

Uses `fast-deep-equal` to compare options - only updates chart when `option` prop actually changes, preventing unnecessary re-renders.

### Event Bus Integration

Static properties for cross-component communication:
- `ReactEcharts.event`: EventMitt instance
- `ReactEcharts.events`: Array of supported events (`['loadEcharts']`)

## Styling

Import the stylesheet:

```tsx
import '@jswork/react-echarts/dist/style.css';
```

CSS classes:
- `.react-echarts` - Main container
- `.react-echarts__spinner` - Loading spinner wrapper

Custom styling example:

```css
.react-echarts {
  width: 100%;
  height: 500px;
}

.react-echarts__spinner img {
  width: 30px; /* smaller spinner */
}
```

## TypeScript Support

Full TypeScript support with exported types:

```typescript
import ReactEcharts, { ReactEchartsProps, useCommand } from '@jswork/react-echarts';
import type { EChartOption, ECharts } from 'echarts';
```

## Advanced Examples

### Custom Script URL

```tsx
<ReactEcharts
  scriptURL="https://your-domain.com/echarts.min.js"
  option={option}
/>
```

### SVG Renderer

```tsx
<ReactEcharts
  initOptions={{ renderer: 'svg' }}
  option={option}
/>
```

### Multiple Charts with Named Instances

```tsx
<ReactEcharts name="chart1" option={option1} />
<ReactEcharts name="chart2" option={option2} />

// Use in other component:
const cmd1 = useCommand('chart1');
const cmd2 = useCommand('chart2');
```

### Direct Chart Instance Access

```tsx
<ReactEcharts
  option={option}
  onReady={({ chart, echarts }) => {
    // Use chart instance directly
    chart.on('click', (params) => {
      console.log('Clicked:', params);
    });

    // Use echarts for advanced features
    echarts.registerTheme('myTheme', {...});
  }}
/>
```

## Module Exports

```typescript
// Default export
export default ReactEcharts;

// Named exports
export { useCommand };
export type { ReactEchartsProps };
```

## Build Outputs

- `dist/main.cjs.js` - CommonJS bundle
- `dist/main.esm.js` - ES Module bundle
- `dist/main.d.ts` - TypeScript declarations
- `dist/style.css` - Compiled styles

## Important Notes

1. **Container Size**: Parent container must have defined dimensions. Chart inherits container size.

2. **Option Updates**: Chart automatically updates when `option` prop changes. Uses deep comparison.

3. **Cleanup**: Component properly disposes chart instance and disconnects ResizeObserver on unmount.

4. **SSR Compatibility**: Script loading happens client-side only. Works with SSR frameworks.

## ECharts Resources

For ECharts configuration options, refer to:
- [ECharts Option Documentation](https://echarts.apache.org/en/option.html)
- [ECharts Examples](https://echarts.apache.org/examples/en/)
